mission.title: vllm: ExLlamaV2: exl2 support
mission.description: ExLlamaV2: exl2 support. Requested by 35+ users.

steps.title: Check current vllm setup
steps.description:
Verify your vllm version and configuration:
```bash
pip show vllm | grep Version
```
This feature requires a working vllm installation.

steps.title: Review vllm configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant vllm configuration:
If is possible ExLlamaV2 is a very fast and good library to Run [LLM](https://mlabonne.github.io/blog/posts/ExLlamaV2_The_Fastest_Library_to_Run%C2%A0LLMs.html)

[ExLlamaV2 Repo](https://github.com/turboderp/exllamav2)

steps.title: Apply the fix for ExLlamaV2: exl2 support
steps.description:
What specifically would you like to see? vLLM already integrates kernels from exllamav2 - see things such as the GPTQ kernels https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/05af6da8d927f70d15ab1ed25b01df3c967ad961/csrc/quantization/gptq/qdq_4.cuh#L2

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "ExLlamaV2: exl2 support" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: What specifically would you like to see? vLLM already integrates kernels from exllamav2 - see things such as the GPTQ kernels https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/05af6da8d927f70d15ab1ed25b01df3c967ad961/csrc/quantization/gptq/qdq_4.cuh#L2

prerequisites.description: A working vllm installation or development environment.
